shared_properties: Hazard art stays on the 16px tile grid used across the prison shaft.
Dark outline weight stays at 1px so damage silhouettes remain readable against the backdrop.
Palette remains low-saturation except for the rust-red damage cue.
differences: Cluster spans 48x16px to present three tightly packed spike tips in one collision group.
Tip pixels brighten toward #8B2020 so the lethal edge reads before contact.
Base stone sockets tie visually into the wall strip instead of floating over the floor.

context.usage: Primary floor hazard that removes one HP on contact with no knockback.

shape.silhouette: Three narrow spike tips rising from a stone base.
shape.collision_read: Tip profile keeps a clean lethal contour across the full cluster width.

acceptance_criteria: Each spike tip reaches a clear 1px apex without anti-aliased blur.
The base sockets visually lock to stone surfaces and do not read as floating props.

reference_images.label: slip_down_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds stone texture breakup, low-key lighting, and vertical shaft composition for the active environment asset.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteBundle aligned to a fixed hazard collision box.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load once during the Loading state and assign the handle to spike hazard prefabs.
runtime_notes: Use Bevy 0.13 with ImagePlugin::default_nearest() so every asset stays pixel-perfect at 4x upscale.
Keep the lethal collision shape in gameplay code so tuning does not require repainting the sprite.
Snap hazard placement to the 16px grid to avoid sub-pixel gaps against floors.
